On this page

Brainstorm E: Content & Outreach Minimal Cadence (WF1 & WF2) ​

Date: 2026-07-06
Owner: Paijo (BerkahKarya)
Status: Minimal Viable Cadence Design

Executive Summary ​

WF1 (Content) and WF2 (Outreach) are the trust layer. Without them, the flywheel can generate money but not compound trust — and trust is what makes WF3/WF4 products sell at a premium.

The trap: both workflows can expand infinitely. This doc defines the minimum viable cadence — enough to keep the trust engine warm without consuming Paijo's bandwidth.

Minimum Viable Cadence (MVP) ​

Rule: 1-2 hours per week total. Automate everything else. ​

WF1: Content Engine — Minimal Cadence ​

Weekly Output (Automated) ​

PlatformFormatVolumeEffort (Paijo)Tool
TikTok/ReelsShort-form video (30-60s)5/week0 min (auto-generated)1ai-content + PostBridge
Twitter/XText + screenshot7/week (1/day)5 min/week (review)1ai-social + PostBridge
LinkedInLong-form insight1/week15 min (edit AI draft)Manual draft, auto-schedule
Telegram channelDaily market update7/week0 min (auto)1ai-trade-cex signal digest

Total Paijo effort: ~20 min/week

Monthly Output (Light Effort) ​

FormatVolumeEffortPurpose
Case study / proof1/month30 minSocial proof for WF4 sales
YouTube video1/month45 min (record, AI edits)SEO + trust
Email newsletter2/month20 min (AI draft, human edit)Nurture leads

Total Paijo effort: ~95 min/month ≈ 25 min/week

Content Pillars (What to Talk About) ​

Pillar 1: Bukti (Proof) — 30%

  • Trading PnL screenshots (real, with disclaimers)
  • Arbitrage deal results (before/after price)
  • Subscriber count growth
  • Tool performance metrics Why: Social proof → WF4 conversions. "This works" > "this should work"

Pillar 2: Edukasi (Education) — 40%

  • "Cara baca chart untuk pemula"
  • "Berapa modal buat mulai trading?"
  • "Tools AI yang gue pakai buat kerja lebih cepat"
  • "Rumus ketahanan pangan: konsep income resilience" Why: Trust-building + SEO + top-of-funnel leads

Pillar 3: Behind-the-scenes — 20%

  • "Building 1ai in public" updates
  • Repo progress screenshots
  • System architecture diagrams (simplified)
  • Failures + lessons learned Why: Community, authenticity, differentiation from fake gurus

Pillar 4: CTA (Call to Action) — 10%

  • "Coba Vilona TradeFX gratis 7 hari"
  • "Join group trading kami"
  • "Ebook mindset trader tersedia di bio" Why: Directly drives WF4 revenue

Content Automation Stack ​

Input → AI Generation → Schedule → Post → Track

Topic ideas (auto from trending keywords)
  ↓
AI drafts (1ai-skills + LLM)
  ↓
Paijo review gate (20 min/week — batch review 7 posts at once)
  ↓
PostBridge scheduler (auto-posts at optimal time)
  ↓
Performance tracking (1ai-social analytics)
  ↓
Feedback loop (what worked → generate more of it)

Key rule: Review in BATCH, not one-by-one. Check 7 posts in 20 min on Monday morning.

WF1 Metrics to Track ​

MetricMinimum (Month 1)Target (Month 3)Scale (Month 6)
Total followers1,0005,00020,000
Avg. engagement rate2%4%5%+
Link clicks/week502001,000
Leads generated/month530150
Revenue attributable to content500k IDR5 juta25 juta

WF2: Outreach Engine — Minimal Cadence ​

WF2 doesn't exist as a repo yet. This defines what it needs. ​

Weekly Outreach Targets ​

Target TypeVolume/WeekChannelEffort (Paijo)
Partnership prospects10 DMsTwitter/Telegram/LinkedIn15 min (template)
Community engagement5 replies/commentsRelevant groups10 min
Affiliate/influencer3 outreachDM + email15 min
Follow-up (warm leads)5 follow-upsDM/email10 min

Total Paijo effort: ~50 min/week

Outreach Templates (Minimal Set) ​

Template 1: Partnership Prospect

Hi [Name],

Lihat konten lo soal [topic] — match banget sama yang BerkahKarya kerjain.

Gue [Paijo], founder BerkahKarya — 1 man, infinite agents. Build tools AI untuk trading dan growth.

Lagi explore kolaborasi: [specific value proposition e.g., cross-promote audience, rev-share on affiliate, co-create content].

Worth 10 menit call? Atau reply sini kalau ada pertanyaan.

— Paijo
berkahkarya.org

Template 2: Affiliate Prospect

Hi [Name],

Lo bikin konten soal [trading/AI/passive income] dan audiens lo keliatan engaged banget.

BerkahKarya punya produk [Vilona TradeFX / 1ai-affiliate] dengan komisi [X]% per referral. Konversi kami sekitar [X]% dari yang trial ke bayar.

Minat cobain? Bisa kasih akses gratis buat review jujur dulu.

— Paijo

Template 3: Community Engagement

[Specific comment/reply that adds value — NO generic replies]
Example: "Setuju, [specific point from their post]. Yang gue temukan di [real scenario]: [specific data/insight]. Coba approach [X] — worked for us."

Outreach Automation Opportunities ​

StepCan Automate?Tool
Prospect discoveryYes1ai-social scraping + scoring
First DM personalizationPartialAI draft → Paijo sends
Follow-up sequenceYes (after manual first contact)CRM sequence
Community monitoring (find threads)YesSocial listening
Partnership CRM trackingYesNotion or simple DB

Rule: First touch = manual (Paijo's name, personal). Follow-ups = automated.

WF2 Target: Partnership Types (by ROI) ​

Tier 1: Revenue-generating partnerships

  • Affiliate marketers who push BerkahKarya products → direct WF4 revenue
  • Content creators who review tools → brand awareness + conversions
  • Trading communities → bulk subscriptions to Vilona TradeFX

Tier 2: Distribution partnerships

  • Indonesian fintech/trading groups → reach new audience
  • AI/tech communities → developer leads for OmniRoute / 1ai products
  • Freelancer communities → potential BerkahKarya service clients

Tier 3: Expertise partnerships

  • Other 1-person businesses → co-create content, share audiences
  • Course creators → bundle their course with BerkahKarya tools
  • Automation consultants → resell 1ai-affiliate to their clients

Minimal WF2 Tech Stack ​

This week (no code):

  • Telegram group: "BerkahKarya Insider" (invite 50 warm contacts)
  • Notion database: Partnership CRM (prospect, status, last contact, deal)
  • Simple outreach tracker (Google Sheet: name, platform, message sent, reply, outcome)

Month 1-2 (light automation):

  • 1ai-social community monitoring script (find threads about trading/AI/Indonesia)
  • Auto-discovery of partnership prospects from follower lists
  • Follow-up reminder system (Hermes DMs Paijo "You haven't followed up with [Name] in 7 days")

Month 3+ (full system):

  • WF2 module in 1ai-social/community/
  • Auto-send follow-ups (with Paijo approval gate for first outreach)
  • Partner performance tracking (referrals, revenue per partner)

Combined WF1+WF2 Weekly Rhythm ​

Monday (20 min):

  • Batch review 7 AI-drafted posts for the week
  • Approve/edit → PostBridge schedules automatically
  • Quick outreach list review: 10 prospects queued

Wednesday (20 min):

  • Review 3 partnership reply/follow-ups
  • Send 3 affiliate outreach DMs (AI-drafted, manual send)
  • Check content analytics (what worked this week?)

Friday (10 min):

  • Check WF1 metrics (followers, clicks, leads)
  • 5 community engagement replies
  • Review any inbound inquiries from content

Total: 50 min/week

The Minimal Viable Content Flywheel ​

This is the smallest loop that actually works:

1. Post educational content (1ai-content auto-generates, Paijo batch-reviews)
   ↓
2. Proof posts (trading screenshots, deal results — auto-pulled from 1ai-trade-cex + marketplace)
   ↓
3. Audience sees proof + education → follows + engages
   ↓
4. CTA in bio + posts → leads try Vilona TradeFX (WF4)
   ↓
5. Converts to subscriber → revenue → Capital Pool
   ↓
6. More capital → more product development → better proof → back to step 2

What makes it "minimal viable": Steps 1 + 2 are 90% automated. Paijo only reviews, doesn't create. But the loop still works because the automation produces real, specific content (not generic AI slop).

WF1+WF2 in the Full Flywheel Context ​

WF1/WF2 enable:

  • WF3 (products sell to warmed-up audience vs. cold)
  • WF4 (subscriptions come from content-generated leads)
  • WF5 (trading content builds credibility for trading products)
  • WF6 (marketplace reputation benefits from personal brand trust)

WF1/WF2 don't need:

  • Capital Pool deposits (they're brand builders, not cash generators)
  • Complex technical infra in Month 1

WF1/WF2 ROI is delayed:

  • Month 1-3: Near-zero direct revenue
  • Month 4-6: Content leads start converting to WF4 subscribers
  • Month 6+: Each piece of content is a permanent lead magnet (compounding)

Anti-Patterns to Avoid ​

❌ Posting daily without a system — leads to burnout and inconsistency
❌ Generic AI content ("Here are 5 tips about trading!") — zero differentiation
❌ Outreach without personalization — gets ignored or damages reputation
❌ Metrics obsession too early — Month 1 is learning, not optimizing
❌ Trying all platforms simultaneously — pick 2-3 and go deep first (TikTok + Telegram)

90-Day Content & Outreach Plan ​

Month 1: Establish ​

  • [ ] Set up PostBridge for TikTok + Telegram automation
  • [ ] Create 5 content templates (proof, education, BTS, CTA, engagement)
  • [ ] Register Telegram "BerkahKarya Insider" group
  • [ ] Manual outreach: 10 prospects/week
  • [ ] Track: followers, engagement rate (baseline)

Month 2: Systematize ​

  • [ ] Batch-review workflow fully operational (20 min/week)
  • [ ] First affiliate partner onboarded
  • [ ] Content analytics feeding back into topic selection
  • [ ] Community monitoring script running
  • [ ] First content-attributed lead converts to WF4 customer

Month 3: Scale ​

  • [ ] 1,000+ followers on primary platform
  • [ ] 3+ affiliate partners active
  • [ ] Content → lead pipeline visible in analytics
  • [ ] WF2 module in 1ai-social starts automating prospecting
  • [ ] Review: which content pillar converts best? Double down.

Conclusion ​

WF1 + WF2 don't need complex infra in Month 1. They need:

  1. Content templates (create once, reuse forever)
  2. Automation backbone (PostBridge already exists — just use it)
  3. Consistent weekly rhythm (50 min/week, no exceptions)
  4. Proof-first content strategy (let real results do the selling)

The trust flywheel starts turning the moment the first piece of real proof (trading result, arbitrage deal, subscriber milestone) goes live. Everything else follows.

Next step: Set up PostBridge scheduling + create 5 content templates this week.
